commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The next action is maintainer compatibility review plus refreshed contributor proof for the current head, not an automated repair.; Cleared: The diff reduces generated-file secret false positives without adding dependency, CI, package, or new secret-execution surfaces; the remaining concerns are compatibility and proof freshness. (sha=ff8809782a6b6d449f6cf81fcc38bf9c021efa98)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes generated env-backed SecretRef provider apiKey values from bare env-var names to `secretref-env:<NAME>` markers, resolves those markers at model-registry request time, and updates audit coverage plus models docs.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +128, Docs 0. Total +148 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source. Current main and v2026.6.1 return bare env-var names for env-backed auth-profile keyRefs, and the audit path flags unknown all-caps string apiKeys in generated `models.json`; I did not run a live CLI repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is maintainer compatibility review plus refreshed contributor proof for the current head, not an automated repair.

target.title: fix(models): keep generated secret refs out of plaintext
target.body:
Fixes #88562.

## Summary

- Persist generated env-backed SecretRef provider auth as `secretref-env:<NAME>` instead of a bare env var name.
- Resolve that marker through `process.env` at model registry request time.
- Treat string SecretRef markers as audit-safe while still flagging unresolved object SecretRefs and true plaintext strings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Per-agent `models.json` generation could persist auth-profile env `keyRef` values as bare strings such as `TENSORIX_API_KEY`, which looked like plaintext to the deep security audit and produced false-positive `PLAINTEXT_FOUND` findings.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/andy/openclaw-88562`, branch `fix/models-json-secret-ref-88562`, commit `ba6d39fa02`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.auth-aliases.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.auth-provenance.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.cloudflare-ai-gateway.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.vercel-ai-gateway.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts src/secrets/audit.test.ts src/agents/models-config.uses-first-github-copilot-profile-env-tokens.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-88562-proof-gBWm15 TENSORIX_API_KEY=redacted-test-token node --import tsx --input-type=module <security-audit-runtime-proof>
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  25 passed (25)
Test Files  9 passed (9)
Tests  64 passed (64)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 22.58s
pnpm check:test-types: passed
git diff --check: passed

proof_state_dir=/tmp/openclaw-88562-proof-gBWm15
models_apiKey=secretref-env:TENSORIX_API_KEY
models_findings=[]
plaintext_models_findings=0

ba6d39fa02 Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> Fix models json secret ref api keys
```

Observed result after fix: A generated provider backed by an auth-profile env `keyRef` now gets `apiKey: "secretref-env:TENSORIX_API_KEY"`; the runtime resolver resolves that marker through `process.env.TENSORIX_API_KEY`, and the security audit runtime reports no `models.json` findings and zero `PLAINTEXT_FOUND` findings for that marker.

What was not tested: The source-tree CLI wrapper requires built `dist/entry.(m)js`, so the proof invoked the patched security audit runtime directly from source rather than `node openclaw.mjs security audit --json`.

## Notes

- Maintainer can modify: enabled.
